stories.summary: Israel has killed more than 700 people in Gaza since last year's US-brokered 'ceasefire'.

stories.summary: Washington and Tehran have both expressed readiness to resume hostilities as Pakistan peace talks remain in limbo after Iran pulled out of the second round of negotiations.

stories.headline: France Sees Up to €6 Billion Budget Impact From Iran War
stories.summary: The war in the Middle East will have an estimated impact on the French budget of as much as €6 billion ($7.1 billion), Finance Minister Roland Lescure said in an interview with RTL radio.

stories.summary: The election on Tuesday is part of a coast-to-coast fight between Republicans and Democrats ahead of the midterms.

Tim Cook names successor as he steps down as Apple CEO
stories.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down from the job that he inherited from the late Steve Jobs, ending a 15-year tenure that saw the company’s market value soar by more than €3.3 trillion during an iPhone-fuelled era of prosperity.

stories.headline: The Onion launches new effort to turn Infowars into parody website
stories.summary: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

stories.headline: Orcel Says UniCredit Will 'Pause' Commerzbank Pursuit If No Control Reached
stories.summary: UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel said he’ll put his yearslong effort to buy Commerzbank on hold if his takeover bid fails to win him control over the lender. “If we do not reach control, we will take a pause, and we will focus on other topics that we have internally,” Orcel said on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Euro Attracts Bullish Options Buyers as Currency Goes Off Script
stories.summary: The euro is the second-best G-10 performer against the dollar over the past month, defying expectations that the energy shock caused by the Middle East war would hit Europe’s economy and drag the currency lower.

stories.headline: Guindos Says ECB Needs to Be Prudent, Keep a Cool Head on Rates
stories.summary: Uncertainty over the Iran war means the European Central Bank should take a measured approach to setting interest rates, according to Vice President Luis de Guindos.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Says Investors Are Focused on Corporate Resilience
stories.summary: JPMorgan Private Bank Global Investment Strategist Madison Faller says the corporate earnings season showed that the "fundamentals remain intact" in spite of the uncertainties around the war in the Middle East. She speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Virginia Redistricting Referendum: What to Watch
stories.summary: In the latest gerrymandering clash before the midterm elections, voters in Virginia will decide on Tuesday whether to approve a map that would give Democrats more House seats.

stories.headline: Digital Finance Providers Seek EU Carve-Out From New Legislation
stories.summary: Digital finance providers want a carve-out from forthcoming European legislation governing distributed ledger technology, arguing that Europe is losing out to the US.

stories.headline: In Full: UniCredit CEO Orcel on His Pursuit of Commerzbank
stories.summary: UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel discusses the status of the lender's bid to buy Commerzbank. The Italian bank is already the largest investor in Commerzbank and currently owns just under 30% in it. Orcel has been trying to buy the German rival for over 18 months, but Commerzbank’s leadership has consistently rebuffed the approach. Orcel speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indian Wind Turbine Giant Says Nation Set to Meet 2030 Target
stories.summary: Suzlon Energy Ltd., India’s largest wind turbine maker, said the nation is on course to hit its goal of 100 gigawatts of wind capacity by the end of the decade, as installations accelerate to meet growing demand for round-the-clock supply.

stories.headline: BOJ Flags Risk to Japan Bonds From Foreign Hedge Funds Unwinding
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan cautioned that a potential unwinding of positions by hedge funds globally may spread to Japan’s bond market.

stories.headline: Carlsberg Dumps Coca-Cola, Bets on Pepsi in Nordic Markets
stories.summary: Carlsberg A/S is preparing a major shift in its soft drinks strategy, replacing Coke with Pepsi across much of Northern Europe in a move set to reshape the region’s beverage market.

stories.headline: Ex-Swedbank CEO Bonnesen Fully Cleared in Dirty Money Case

stories.headline: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon

stories.summary: Stocks rose modestly as traders waited to see if Iran will join talks that could secure a Middle East peace deal and help restore oil flows. The dollar advanced and Treasuries wavered ahead of Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

stories.headline: Indonesia Stocks Slide After MSCI Delays High-Stakes Review
stories.summary: Shares tied to some of Indonesia’s wealthiest individuals dragged the equity benchmark lower after MSCI extended its review to June and warned that tightly held stocks could be dropped from its indexes.

stories.headline: Kansas Mayor Who Voted Illegally Will Not Serve Time in Prison
stories.summary: Joe Ceballos, a green card holder who resigned as Coldwater’s mayor after being charged, said he did not know that he had to be a citizen to vote.

stories.headline: Victory Giant Surges in Debut After Biggest Hong Kong Listing This Year
